heading: Purple Heart: members killed or wounded in attacks by foreign terrorist organizations
label: 1129a Purple Heart: members killed or wounded in attacks by foreign terrorist organizations
notes: Retroactive Effective Date and Application, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–291, div. A, title V, § 571(a)(2)128 Stat. 3387Dec. 19, 2014“(A).—Effective dateThe amendments made by paragraph (1) [enacting this section] shall take effect as of .September 11, 2001“(B).—Review of certain previous incidentsThe Secretary concerned shall undertake a review of each death or wounding of a member of the Armed Forces that occurred between , and the date of the enactment of this Act [] under circumstances that could qualify as being the result of an attack described in , United States Code (as added by paragraph (1)), to determine whether the death or wounding qualifies as a death or wounding resulting from an attack by a foreign terrorist organization for purposes of the award of the Purple Heart pursuant to such section (as so added).September 11, 2001Dec. 19, 2014section 1129a of title 10“(C).—Actions following reviewIf the death or wounding of a member of the Armed Forces reviewed under subparagraph (B) is determined to qualify as a death or wounding resulting from an attack by a foreign terrorist organization as described in , United States Code (as so added), the Secretary concerned shall take appropriate action under such section to award the Purple Heart to the member.section 1129a of title 10“(D).—Secretary concerned definedIn this paragraph, the term ‘Secretary concerned’ has the meaning given that term in , United States Code.”section 101(a)(9) of title 10

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: For purposes of the award of the Purple Heart, the Secretary concerned shall treat a member of the armed forces described in subsection (b) in the same manner as a member who is killed or wounded as a result of an international terrorist attack against the United States.

A member described in this subsection is a member on active duty who was killed or wounded in an attack by a foreign terrorist organization in circumstances where the death or wound is the result of an attack targeted on the member due to such member’s status as a member of the armed forces, unless the death or wound is the result of willful misconduct of the member.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual or entity was in communication with the foreign terrorist organization before the attack; and

children.label: B
children.text: the attack was inspired or motivated by the foreign terrorist organization.

children.heading: .—Foreign Terrorist Organization Defined
children.label: c .—Foreign Terrorist Organization Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “foreign terrorist organization” means an entity designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the Secretary of State pursuant to section 219 of the Immigration and Nationality Act ().8 U.S.C. 1189
